sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: 12 months of operations, maintenance, and improvements for the Eternl non-custodial Cardano light wallet, including frontend/backend work, infrastructure, support, administration, audits, and day-to-day operations.

Deliverables: Frontend maintenance and cross-platform development for Chrome extension, web/PWA, iOS, Android, and beta channel, Backend infrastructure and operations including multi-region servers, Cardano nodes, DBSync/indexers, application servers, metadata aggregation, monitoring, alerting, and incident response, Governance tooling enhancements including DRep dashboards, proposal browsers, in-wallet voting, and proposal creation, User support through Discord ticketing, Telegram, public channels, ecosystem project support, wiki updates, and educational videos, Periodic independent audits of treasury fund use and implementation of oversight metrics, Pro plan rollout for personal and company users with on-chain recording of paid plans and reporting on Pro earnings, Repayment and donation mechanism to return surplus funds or later Pro-plan surplus to the treasury under stated conditions
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Eternl requests ₳2,350,000, valued at about $0.1787 per Ada, to fund a 12-month delivery period from August 2026 to July 2027.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal says Eternl costs around $420,000 per year and funds 6.0 FTE distributed across 10 contributors at $70,000 per FTE.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposed budget allocates 55% to frontend, 25% to backend, 5% to support, 13% to admin, and 2% to audits.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Eternl says it will convert up to $420,000 worth of Ada into stablecoins, place it in a public company wallet, and return any Ada above that amount to the treasury.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, medium materiality): The proposal states that an unaffiliated independent party will audit treasury fund receipt, custody, conversion, spending, balances, Pro-plan income relevant to repayment, and repayments in February 2027 and August 2027.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Eternl claims an install base of about 100,000 browser-extension users and about 30,000 Android and iOS users.
missing: Independent evidence for: Eternl claims an install base of about 100,000 browser-extension users and about 30,000 Android and iOS users.
Independent evidence for: Eternl claims 10-18% of Cardano mainnet transactions are conducted via Eternl.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unknown (stale snapshot)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Eternl requests ₳2,350,000, valued at about $0.1787 per Ada, to fund a 12-month delivery period from August 2026 to July 2027." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Eternl claims an install base of about 100,000 browser-extension users and about 30,000 Android and iOS users." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
